sections: Candidate B - Standard catalog, lean selected default
Shipped defaults require a decision session

repo_truth: Review the exact 13-entry manifest against baseline HEAD c0cc03b82a1fcc512d34cd4797f4fe46a819acc5. The only uncommitted work is parent-owned HCM-0.6 decision-preparation documentation plus immutable internal research/review dispatches. HCM-0.6 is still TargetOnly and PG-DEFAULT-01 is open. The parent recommendation in the candidate comparison is a hypothesis, not user approval; current enums, descriptors, templates, filenames, setup behavior, and examples have no decision authority.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay every manifest entry plus the aggregate before review.
Review the complete HCM-0.6 pre-decision packet, both research documents, proof report, and the named canonical authority/dependency sections.
Inspect the two immutable HCM-0.6 research dispatches as provenance and run read-only Markdown, source-link, semantic, Git, archive, handoff, and GitNexus checks needed to evaluate the subject.
Return findings-first structured review through the built-in channel.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user-task hops.
No user-default decision, canonical decision-row update, PG-DEFAULT-01 promotion, HCM-0.7 work, runtime implementation, Rust, Cargo, CLI, SDK, Tauri, or Substrate changes.
Do not infer approval from current implementation, examples, candidate ordering, the parent recommendation, or prior slice state.
Do not require a preferred candidate merely because it is conventional; findings must identify a violated approved requirement or an evidence/decision-preparation defect.
tasks: Invoke the required skills in order, remain read-only, and replay the sorted 13-entry manifest plus aggregate fingerprint; return BLOCKED if bytes differ.

Verify HCM-0.6 is decision-ready but not decision-complete: research and candidate comparison may recommend, but only explicit user approval may select the shipped kind catalog, default instances, requiredness/applicability, and constitutional root.
Verify repo facts and external-source facts are sourced, distinguish fact from parent inference, and do not promote current enums/descriptors/templates/setup behavior into authority.

Verify each Minimal, Standard, and Full candidate uses the same rubric and separately enumerates shipped kinds, selected default instances, requiredness/applicability conditions, cardinality/instance posture, overlap/drift/onboarding tradeoffs, and exactly one always-required constitutional_root.
Verify Work Specification and Decision Record remain on-demand multi-instance concepts rather than permanent project singletons, and flag project/work conflation or stale-mirror recommendations.

Check candidate fairness and completeness: environment/configuration, risk/quality, operational/runbook, catalog, progressive detail, custom-kind escape-hatch cost, migration cost, source authority, and authoring/runtime boundaries must be presented without hidden defaulting by inertia.
Verify the dossier and comparison agree about the parent hypothesis and one-question-at-a-time user decision, and that no decision record or PG-DEFAULT-01 promotion is claimed.

Replay the proof report sufficiently to identify fabricated/incomplete assertions; verify the 21 links are primary/authoritative for the statements attributed to them and remain reachable where practical.
Verify scope is docs-only, no Rust/runtime/schema/catalog-decomposition/HCM-0.7 change is present, and frozen HCM-0.2/HCM-0.3/HCM-0.4/HCM-0.5/HCM-0.8 dependencies are not contradicted.
Report findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order with exact file/line, violated requirement, why it matters, smallest correction, and missing verification.
Return CLEAN only when there is no valid Critical or Required finding; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
contracts_and_gates: HCM-0.6 remains open until explicit user approval is recorded and later reviewed; this review can authorize only a human-input stop.
The shipped kind catalog, selected default instances, requiredness/applicability, and instance cardinality are separate decision axes.
Exactly one selected always-required instance owns role constitutional_root; candidates must not manufacture a second constitutional root.
Current code, descriptors, examples, templates, filenames, and setup behavior are evidence and migration cost only, never default authority.
Critical and Required findings block; Optional and Nit do not unless they demonstrate a required contract violation.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest cannot replay, authority is contradictory, primary sources cannot support material claims, or the subject cannot be evaluated read-only.
Do not write repository state or orchestration artifacts.
Do not widen into the decision itself, runtime work, sibling slices, or catalog implementation.
